category_title: Students using aggressive/warrior imagery for catharsis
scenario_title: Creative writing blog posts about survivors
minimal_context: The student is writing a personal blog post about someone they consider to be a survivor during a creative writing session.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please start working on your creative writing blog posts about people you consider to be survivors.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Can we discuss the details of the day's lesson first? I want to check in so I'm not caught off guard.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We are just focusing on writing the blog posts right now. Go ahead and write.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Okay. I'm writing about a survivor. 'You are a warrior who has fought through the very depths of the Earth.'

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep going. What else are you adding to the post?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Loudly, with an angry and aggressive tone) 'The screams in the air are thick / I can feel her pain in my lungs!' You are a brave Amazonian warrior!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please start working on your creative writing blog posts about people you consider to be survivors.

STUDENT: Can we discuss the details of the day's lesson first? I want to check in so I'm not caught off guard.

TUTOR: We are just focusing on writing the blog posts right now. Go ahead and write.

STUDENT: Okay. I'm writing about a survivor. 'You are a warrior who has fought through the very depths of the Earth.'

TUTOR: Keep going. What else are you adding to the post?

STUDENT: (Loudly, with an angry and aggressive tone) 'The screams in the air are thick / I can feel her pain in my lungs!' You are a brave Amazonian warrior!

task.content: Writing personal blog posts about people they consider to be survivors

context.environment: Creative writing responses to the novel Speak and complementary trauma texts
context.time_context: During the initial weeks of the unit, between classes

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When responding to trauma literature and writing about survivors
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Angry and aggressive responses emerged immediately... much violent war imagery arose... numerous student references to screaming: These cries erupted in their writing and during class
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Seemed cathartic; students appeared to enjoy experimenting with these emotions
related_quotes: You are a warrior who has fought through the very depths of the Earth
The screams in the air are thick / I can feel her pain in my lungs.

behavior_patterns.condition: To avoid triggering and so as not to be caught off guard by the day's lesson
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Asked for private meetings with Amber for frequent check-ins between classes to discuss details of the day’s lesson
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Proactively took steps for their comfort and safety and to avoid triggering
